name: OWASP Top 10
description: OWASP Top 10 Web Application Security Risks (2021) - critical web app vulnerabilities

requirements.requirement_text: Access control enforces policy such that users cannot act outside of their intended permissions. Failures lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction.
requirements.expected_evidence: Access control policy, authorization matrix, penetration test results for access control
requirements.recommendation_text: Implement deny-by-default access controls, enforce ownership checks server-side, and test for IDOR and privilege escalation.

requirements.requirement_text: Deny access by default and enforce least privilege for all users and API endpoints.
requirements.expected_evidence: Least privilege policy, API authorization configuration
requirements.recommendation_text: Configure all endpoints to deny by default and grant only required permissions per role.

requirements.requirement_text: Sensitive data is protected at rest and in transit using strong cryptography. Failures include transmitting or storing sensitive data in cleartext.
requirements.expected_evidence: Encryption policy, TLS configuration, data classification with encryption requirements
requirements.recommendation_text: Encrypt all sensitive data at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+) and disable weak cipher suites.

requirements.requirement_text: Classify data processed by the application and apply appropriate cryptographic protections.
requirements.expected_evidence: Data classification policy, encryption implementation documentation
requirements.recommendation_text: Classify all application data and apply encryption proportional to sensitivity.

requirements.requirement_text: User-supplied data is validated, sanitized, and never interpreted as commands or queries. SQL, OS, LDAP, and other injection flaws are prevented.
requirements.expected_evidence: Secure coding standards, SAST/DAST results, parameterized query usage
requirements.recommendation_text: Use parameterized queries/ORMs, validate all input, and run injection-focused security testing.

requirements.requirement_text: All database queries use parameterized statements or safe ORM patterns.
requirements.expected_evidence: Code review records, ORM configuration, SAST scan results
requirements.recommendation_text: Eliminate dynamic SQL concatenation; enforce parameterized queries across all data access layers.

requirements.requirement_text: Security is integrated into the design and architecture phase. Threat modeling and secure design patterns are applied before implementation.
requirements.expected_evidence: Threat models, security design reviews, secure architecture documentation
requirements.recommendation_text: Conduct threat modeling for all new features and apply secure design patterns from the design phase.

requirements.requirement_text: Systems are securely configured with hardened defaults, minimal attack surface, and disabled unnecessary features.
requirements.expected_evidence: Hardening baselines, configuration management, security scan results
requirements.recommendation_text: Apply CIS/vendor hardening guides, remove default accounts, and automate configuration compliance scanning.

requirements.requirement_text: Security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options) are properly configured on all HTTP responses.
requirements.expected_evidence: HTTP security header configuration, security header scan results
requirements.recommendation_text: Deploy comprehensive security headers on all web application responses.

requirements.requirement_text: Components (libraries, frameworks, OS) are inventoried, monitored for vulnerabilities, and kept up to date.
requirements.expected_evidence: Dependency inventory, SCA scan results, patch management policy
requirements.recommendation_text: Maintain SBOM, scan dependencies continuously, and patch critical vulnerabilities within defined SLAs.

requirements.requirement_text: Authentication mechanisms confirm user identity and resist credential stuffing, brute force, and session hijacking.
requirements.expected_evidence: Authentication policy, MFA deployment, account lockout configuration
requirements.recommendation_text: Enforce MFA, strong password policies, rate limiting on login, and secure session management.

requirements.requirement_text: Multi-factor authentication is required for all privileged and administrative accounts.
requirements.expected_evidence: MFA policy for admins, MFA enrollment records
requirements.recommendation_text: Require MFA for all administrative accounts without exception.

requirements.requirement_text: Software updates and CI/CD pipelines are protected against unauthorized modification. Integrity of data and code is verified.
requirements.expected_evidence: CI/CD pipeline security, signed build artifacts, integrity verification
requirements.recommendation_text: Sign and verify build artifacts, secure CI/CD pipelines, and validate third-party component integrity.

requirements.requirement_text: Security-relevant events are logged, monitored, and alerted on. Logs are protected from tampering.
requirements.expected_evidence: Logging policy, SIEM configuration, alert rules, log retention policy
requirements.recommendation_text: Log all authentication, authorization, and input validation failures; integrate with SIEM with real-time alerting.

requirements.requirement_text: Failed login attempts and access control violations are logged and monitored.
requirements.expected_evidence: Security event logging configuration, monitoring dashboard
requirements.recommendation_text: Configure logging and alerting for all failed authentication and authorization events.

requirements.requirement_text: The application validates and sanitizes URLs and prevents server-side requests to unintended destinations.
requirements.expected_evidence: SSRF prevention controls, URL validation implementation, network segmentation
requirements.recommendation_text: Validate and allowlist outbound URLs, segment application servers from internal networks.

requirements.requirement_text: CORS policy is restrictive and does not allow unauthorized cross-origin access to sensitive resources.
requirements.expected_evidence: CORS configuration documentation
requirements.recommendation_text: Restrict CORS to trusted origins only; never use wildcard CORS with credentials.

requirements.requirement_text: Cryptographic keys and secrets are managed securely and never hardcoded in source code.
requirements.expected_evidence: Secrets management solution, code scanning for hardcoded secrets
requirements.recommendation_text: Use a secrets manager; scan code for hardcoded credentials; rotate keys regularly.

requirements.requirement_text: Rate limiting is implemented on authentication, API, and resource-intensive endpoints.
requirements.expected_evidence: Rate limiting configuration, API gateway throttling policies
requirements.recommendation_text: Deploy rate limiting on all public endpoints, especially authentication and data export APIs.

requirements.requirement_text: A process exists to remove unused dependencies, features, and accounts.
requirements.expected_evidence: Dependency audit records, decommissioning procedures
requirements.recommendation_text: Regularly audit and remove unused libraries, services, and user accounts.

requirements.requirement_text: Session tokens are invalidated on logout and expire after a defined idle timeout.
requirements.expected_evidence: Session management policy, session timeout configuration
requirements.recommendation_text: Invalidate sessions server-side on logout and enforce idle session timeouts.

requirements.requirement_text: Deserialization of untrusted data is prevented or strictly controlled.
requirements.expected_evidence: Deserialization controls, secure coding standards
requirements.recommendation_text: Avoid deserializing untrusted data; use safe serialization formats like JSON with schema validation.

requirements.requirement_text: Internal network metadata services (e.g., cloud metadata endpoints) are blocked from application access.
requirements.expected_evidence: Network egress controls, metadata endpoint protection
requirements.recommendation_text: Block access to cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254) and internal IP ranges from application servers.
